source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Zinc Bromide Discharge, Garden Banks Block 216

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Garden Banks Block 216

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Zinc Bromide Discharge
actions_taken: ROV stabbed into subsea production tree control module to partially close safety valves
ROV returned to rig to obtain correct tool
ROV returned and fully closed SV1 and SV2 safety valves
Bore protector run to isolate other potential leaks
event.summary: During permanent abandonment operations on Well #004 at Garden Banks Block 216, approximately 22 barrels of zinc bromide (ZnBr2) were discharged into offshore waters. The discharge originated from high pressure vent lines connected to subsea production tree safety valves (SV1 and SV2) that were left in the open position after tubing hanger retrieval. The incident was discovered during ROV monitoring of pressure testing operations. No injuries occurred.

hazards.name: Pressurized zinc bromide in subsea production tree vent lines
Open safety valve ports on subsea production tree

threats.name: Safety valves left in open position after tubing hanger retrieval
Safety valves omitted from Plan of Action drawings

consequences.name: Discharge of zinc bromide into offshore waters
Regulatory non-compliance and incident of noncompliance issued

controls.name: Tubing hanger retrieval procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safety valves are closed after tubing hanger removal
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent hazardous condition

supporting_text: Incomplete tubing hanger retrieval procedures that did not specify that SV1 and SV2 safety valves on the subsea production tree vent lines needed to be closed after removing tubing hanger

controls.name: Plan of Action (POA) drawings and documentation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure all critical components are identified in operational drawings
controls.lod_basis: Documentation control to prevent omission of critical safety components

supporting_text: The SV1 and SV2 safety valves were not included in the Plan of Action (POA) drawings when the subsea production tree and control module drawings were merged

controls.name: ROV monitoring and intervention
controls.barrier_role: Detection and manual intervention to identify and stop discharge
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring and remote intervention capability

supporting_text: The ZnBr2 leak was first discovered while the remote operated vehicle (ROV) was monitoring the subsea production tree
The ROV stabbed into the subsea production tree control module; however, it was only able to partially close the two safety valves since it was not equipped with the appropriate tool

controls.name: Stripping tank monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detection of discharge volume and rate
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring system to detect fluid discharge

supporting_text: At 1945 hour while monitoring the stripping tank, it was determined that approximately 16 barrels of 15.5 pounds per gallon ZnBr2 had been discharged into offshore waters with a loss rate of approximately 3 barrels per hour

controls.name: Subsea production tree safety valves (SV1 and SV2)
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of pressurized fluid in vent lines

supporting_text: the subsea production tree high pressure vent safety valves that were left in the open position

the ZnBr2 discharge was originating from the high pressure vent lines that are connected to the subsea production tree safety valves (SV1 and SV2) tubing hanger ports that were left in the open position after retrieving the tubing hanger

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
